satire_attempt: The staff engineer, having internalised BG 2.69, sits in the architecture review with a contemplative expression. She has read no appendix; she is genuinely zoned out. She speaks once, asks an oblique question about 'the deeper substrate', and the room nods sagely. The chief-of-staff codes her as 'senior wisdom'. Three months later, the migration ships, hot-spots under holiday traffic, and the post-mortem reveals that the only person in the room who could have caught the partitioning issue had been awake to a different field — specifically, her browser tabs were on a Twitter thread about a competitor's funding round.
verdict_reason: My satire trades on the analog's awake-to-substrate framing by replacing the substrate with vacancy. But the verse's actual claim — that the inversion is recognisable by what the silent engineer is AWAKE to (the technical truth, the load-test results, the partitioning hot-spot, the replica-lag mismatch) — is exactly what the analog protects against my mockery. The falsifiability clause is on the nose: 'Most quiet engineers are simply unprepared.' My zoned-out engineer is not the muni; she is the unprepared one the falsifiability clause names. The verse's reading requires the technical-truth perception to be active, falsifiably so (her two flat sentences about the partitioning hot-spot and replica-lag mismatch are the artifact). Pass.
